mobile_ap.summary: Apple and Broadcom expanded their partnership with a $30 billion deal to produce over 15 billion chips domestically, reinforcing Apple's shift toward a U.S. semiconductor ecosystem. Samsung is aggressively competing on pricing, raising 5nm and 4nm wafer prices 15% while simultaneously developing a 4nm AI accelerator SoC for PCs and accelerating three generations of Exynos SoC releases to reduce Qualcomm dependence.

hpc_datacenter.summary: Chinese AI firms are pivoting domestically, with 46% of AI chip budgets now directed to Huawei and local alternatives, surpassing Nvidia for the first time in budget allocation. A critical talent shortage looms, potentially reaching 150,000 deficits by 2030 for U.S. expansion, while SK Hynix, Samsung, and Nvidia are aggressively recruiting globally—with SK Hynix removing educational requirements and opening R&D centers in Vietnam, and Nvidia establishing a research hub in Israel; Tesla has abandoned Dojo supercomputer development in favor of Nvidia and Samsung chips.

custom_soc.summary: Meta will begin manufacturing its in-house Iris AI chip in September, targeting 14 gigawatts of computing power and doubling capacity by 2027. Chinese AI labs DeepSeek and Zhipu are developing proprietary inference-focused chips to address computing bottlenecks, while Arteris partnered with IC-Link by imec to accelerate AI and HPC silicon development, and Microsoft's custom AI chip is entering datacenter deployments alongside continued Nvidia and AMD presence.

foundry.summary: TSMC and Samsung implemented foundry price hikes amid AI demand outpacing supply, with Japan's Rapidus aggressively targeting 2nm pricing at ¥3.5M per wafer to compete; TSMC's ecosystem advantage spans 93,000 certified IPs while Samsung deepened collaboration with Cadence on 2nm and 3D-IC technology for AI infrastructure. NVIDIA's next-generation Rosa CPU is expected to adopt TSMC's A16 process with back-side power delivery targeting 2028, and TSMC's photonic interconnect capacity will surge over 30-fold in three years.

packaging.summary: Advanced packaging growth is being mapped by SEMI and TechSearch International amid surging demand from semiconductor and automation markets. EU-Japan semiconductor innovation projects are receiving funding, while batch tank systems, encapsulating resins, and test-and-validation markets forecast strong growth through 2035 driven by advanced packaging and miniaturization requirements tied to semiconductor expansion.
